steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Canadian astronomers detected a striking and unusual phenomenon occurring in the constellation Lepus, 160 million light-years from ______ explosion of a massive star, also known as a supernova..." The left side is a complete independent clause ending at "Earth." The right side is a noun phrase that identifies what the phenomenon is. The blank must link a complete clause to a noun phrase that explains it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Earth. The': Creates a sentence fragment — "The explosion of a massive star..." would stand alone as a noun phrase with no verb. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Earth, it was the': Inserts a pronoun subject ("it was"), creating a comma splice between two independent clauses. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Earth: the': A colon after a complete clause correctly introduces a noun phrase that explains what the phenomenon is. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Earth; the': A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "the explosion of a massive star..." is a noun phrase, not an independent clause. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
